ab: Duuq i maqtu ata ihaan laqbing tu minaunin mapasaduu?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: Uu, maqtu. I’isaq ata mapasaduu?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: Maqtu ihaan kaku’ ta? Nalu’in ata
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: Azuq munhaan u’anpukan ta, namainuk aak hulus Bunun.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: Manau’az naam asang ti ka dandan, maaq i kapupuaqin a munzakuana tantungu!
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: Na’asang tu sauhaan mintalaban maltatala suu, natalmadia’ang tu!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: Azuqin ihaan kulisimasu mun’iti i!
ch: W: 那你聖誕節的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Christmas!

ab: Namaupinti’in i! Namaltatala aak suu.
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: Mavia aas tu namudaan mulumaq i?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回家裡呢?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: Mulumaq aak min’uni masnanava.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: Mavia nitu ihaan asang-daingaz ti min’uni masnanava i?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: Aupa masalpu aak inaak asang, asa masaunbaas i asang tu sinkadaidaz zakuan.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: Maqansiap aas matindun, simaq maisnava suu tu?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: Sia inaak bai maisnava zaku, mastaan aipa tu maqansiap matindun ihaan asang naam ti.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: Mavia aas tu asa mapasnava matindun hulus i?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: Maaq a matindun hulus una inaam tu vungka’, asauk aipi mapalmamaupa.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: Masihal a davus-Bunun un qudan.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: Maaq aipi a inaak sinkai’uni tu davus.
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: Tutuza aas tu masaihuu, talmadia aas kadavus ti?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: Nitu talmadia, musqa taltiun. ngausun a dulaz masasngul, a pitdazaunin, susan i tamul haan pakiung a, a mindavusin.
ch: W:不用，大約三天就好了。把糯米和白米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about three days. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
